decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the Greasefang Parhelion combo to generate value and overwhelm the opponent. It includes powerful cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss, Parhelion II, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain board presence. The deck also includes cards like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk to provide additional threats and support for the combo. By efficiently controlling the board and leveraging the synergies between the cards, this deck aims to outpace and outlast the opponent to achieve victory.

This deck focuses on using the combination of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful and synergistic engine. By controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse, while also disrupting the opponent's hand with Thoughseize, the deck aims to set up a winning board state with Monument to Endurance. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Iron-Shield Elf provide added protection and utility to ensure the combo can go off smoothly and efficiently.

This deck focuses on controlling the game with a variety of removal spells and versatile creatures, while using Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability to gain card advantage and consistently find powerful cards from all five colors. The inclusion of Bring to Light allows for flexibility in finding key spells, while the companion Yorion, Sky Nomad provides extra value through blinking permanents. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's multicolor requirements, with a mix of shock lands, triomes, and special lands like Sunbillow Verge and Spara's Headquarters. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents in a grindy control game.

The key strategy of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient creature-based offense. By utilizing cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent's board while also disrupting their game plan. With mana ramp from Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, the deck can quickly deploy threats such as Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Ouroboroid and Toski, Bearer of Secrets provides additional versatility and resilience in combat situations.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combination of cards involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a strong board presence and overwhelm opponents. The archetype is built around generating value from sacrificing creatures and utilizing various synergies to outmaneuver and outpace the opponent. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, there is a mix of lands and support cards to ensure consistency and efficiency in executing the combo.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the synergy between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. By using cards like Formidable Speaker and Esika's Chariot to support the main combo pieces, the deck aims to control the board and generate value while setting up for a game-winning play. Thoughtseize and Cache Grab help to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Witherbloom Command and Overlord of the Balemurk provide additional support and flexibility in various situations. The deck focuses on efficiently executing its combo strategy to secure victory.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and disruptive tactics to overwhelm opponents. The key strategy revolves around utilizing efficient mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to ramp into powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. Collected Company offers additional creature synergy, while cards like Skyclave Apparition and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a mix of strong creatures and disruption, this deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain board control to secure victories in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matchups.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to aggressively control the battlefield using low-cost spells and creatures to deal direct damage to the opponent while maintaining board presence. With cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, the deck aims to chip away at the opponent's life total quickly and efficiently. The deck also utilizes cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the opponent's creatures and clear the way for its own attacks. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells, while also benefiting from powerful synergy between its cards. By playing creatures like Thalia and Archon of Emeria to slow down the opponent and utilizing mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to ramp into threats quickly, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain a strong board presence. Cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition provide additional value and versatility, allowing for flexibility in adapting to different board states.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to outvalue and outlast opponents by using a combination of board wipes, card draw, and utility lands. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion gives additional value to the deck by providing a powerful creature that can also act as a free inclusion. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly building up its own resources to secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with fast, cheap creatures and burn spells to control the board and deal direct damage. It utilizes cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to apply pressure early on, with spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to clear blockers and push damage through. The deck's mana base is built for efficiency, allowing it to consistently cast its spells and maintain consistency in its aggressive game plan. The goal is to win the game as quickly as possible through a mix of creature and burn damage.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II as the main win conditions. The deck also includes cards like Bitter Triumph and Witherbloom Command to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also features mana-fixing lands like Concealed Courtyard and Temple Garden to ensure smooth gameplay. The overall strategy is to efficiently ramp up and protect the combo pieces until the winning combo can be executed.

The deck focuses on resource denial and disruption while also maintaining board control. It utilizes a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and mana acceleration to control the pace of the game. Creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Ouroboroid provide additional value while cards like Darkbore Pathway and Mutavault help in maintaining board presence. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse adds a strong win condition to the deck. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through a combination of removal, disruption, and powerful creature threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a solid board presence. It utilizes removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck also has mana ramp in the form of Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to accelerate its game plan. Additionally, it includes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Ouroboroid to pressure the opponent and close out the game. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of disruption and threats.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and using a combination of counter spells and removal to maintain board control. The addition of Yorion as a companion allows for additional value by blinking permanents like Mutavault and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent with card advantage and versatile threats, while also utilizing lands like Darkslick Shores and Watery Grave to fix mana and ensure consistency in the early game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using efficient creatures and burn spells. The deck aims to control the board with cheap spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage while maintaining pressure with Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. Academic Dispute serves as both removal and card draw, while Boomerang Basics and Brazen Borrower provide additional disruption. The mana base is streamlined to ensure consistency in casting spells and maximizing damage output. The deck thrives on tempo, utilizing creatures with prowess to snowball into a quick victory.
